dataPolicy.publicRecord: A minimized manifest or report may contain pseudonymous roles, content-addressed evidence references, measured outcomes, findings, limitations, and signoffs only.
dataPolicy.privateRecord: Raw photos, telemetry, participant identity, credentials, provider payloads, and signed originals belong in an access-controlled evidence store governed by consent, retention, export, deletion, and legal-hold policy.

milestones.title: Independent builder acceptance
prerequisites: The participant has no repository knowledge and receives only the supported product entry point and acceptance script.
The selected catalog rows are reviewed, revision-pinned, cited, and license-classified.
QA-002 remains green on the exact product revision under test.
A support contact and safe data-handling notice are available.
stopConditions: The participant needs repository access, source edits, hidden fixture knowledge, or direct database manipulation.
A validator, ownership, review, citation, license, privacy, or authorization gate is bypassed.
A secret or raw participant identifier appears in the acceptance manifest.
The tested deployment changes after the run begins without starting a new run.

steps.title: Open from the supported product entry
steps.instruction: Give the participant the documented URL or packaged entry point, no repository instructions, and record whether they can identify the starting action.

steps.title: Configure reviewed catalog parts
steps.instruction: Select and equip reviewed, cited, revision-pinned parts and record every compatibility explanation or confusing state.

steps.title: Validate and understand repair guidance
steps.instruction: Run the sovereign validator, resolve or explain diagnostics through supported UI, and retain the exact versioned report.

steps.title: Share the admitted design
steps.instruction: Create a read-only share and prove anonymous viewing while private owner routes remain inaccessible.

steps.title: Produce BOM and lawful export
steps.instruction: Generate the BOM and export package; inspect SKUs, prices, citations, review state, attribution, and restricted-geometry fallbacks.

steps.title: Review physical and purchasing correctness
steps.instruction: Compare configured facts and artifacts against their cited sources and record correctness findings without silently correcting the evidence.

steps.title: Complete independent debrief and signoff
steps.instruction: Record task completion, elapsed time, confusion, assistance, failures, limitations, and independent participant attestation.

milestones.title: Real photo-to-reviewed-part acceptance
prerequisites: The photographed motor is real, owned or authorized, non-sensitive, and consented for this processing purpose.
The provider sandbox, budget, retention behavior, timeout, and deletion path are known.
D13 thresholds and catalog admission rules are unchanged and machine-enforced.
stopConditions: Ownership or photoscan-processing consent is missing, stale, or withdrawn.
The provider exceeds its time, byte, cost, retry, or destination boundary.
A failed D13 result is relabeled parametric or a row bypasses human review.
Raw photos, credentials, or private provider payloads are copied into Git evidence.

steps.title: Verify authority and capture protocol
steps.instruction: Verify ownership and purpose-specific consent, then record the reproducible single-image or multiview capture protocol.

steps.title: Run the real reconstruction provider
steps.instruction: Execute TRELLIS or COLMAP through the bounded sandbox adapter and retain provider, model, latency, cost, retry, and redaction evidence.

steps.title: Verify cache and artifact integrity
steps.instruction: Prove source-keyed cache behavior, object integrity, provenance, and safe retry or cancellation behavior.

steps.title: Measure D13 primitive refit
steps.instruction: Measure fit coverage and Hausdorff residual; retain mesh-class fallback when either threshold fails.

steps.title: Complete owner alignment
steps.instruction: Align scale, axes, datum ports, and envelope in the supported Studio flow and preserve the editable candidate.

steps.title: Review citations, license, and physical facts
steps.instruction: A catalog reviewer inspects identity, dimensions, mass, citations, confidence, license, and source conflicts before approval.

steps.title: Equip and validate the reviewed component
steps.instruction: Equip the immutable reviewed revision in a real model, validate it, and record BOM/export behavior.

steps.title: Assess SLO and data lifecycle
steps.instruction: Compare end-to-end time and cost to the declared SLO and exercise or verify the photo/export/deletion lifecycle without overclaiming provider erasure.

milestones.title: Real training and browser-policy acceptance
prerequisites: The admitted model, exact task/tensor/bundle majors, observation/action contract, randomization grid, success threshold, recipe, seeds, hardware, execution device, and budget are frozen before training.
The real SB3/MuJoCo provider path and output retention/deletion behavior are configured.
Ground-truth-only policies remain non-exportable.
stopConditions: Task, scorecard threshold, seed policy, or evaluation corpus changes after results are observed.
Training data lacks authority or crosses its consent/retention boundary.
The exported artifact lacks lineage, I/O header, estimator evidence, or integrity hash.
Browser playback uses a fixture controller instead of the recorded ONNX artifact.

Hardware inventory is presented as accelerator execution, a backend fallback is hidden, simulated vehicle energy is presented as host consumption, or an adapter-rating upper bound is presented as measured energy.

steps.title: Freeze task and reproducibility inputs
steps.instruction: Record model hash, task/tensor/bundle versions, simulator, estimator, observation/action layout, recipe, seeds, randomization, threshold, safe hardware inventory, requested/resolved device with fallback policy, power-evidence method, and budget.

steps.title: Run the real training pipeline
steps.instruction: Execute the bounded SB3/MuJoCo training adapter with progress, intentional interruption plus validated resume, timeout, resource, latency, exact backend, host-energy method/nonclaim, and cost evidence.

steps.title: Reproduce held-out evaluation
steps.instruction: Evaluate on the frozen scorecard corpus and retain per-seed outcomes, failure cases, and aggregate statistics.

steps.title: Export the actual policy artifact
steps.instruction: Export ONNX with versioned I/O header, model/task lineage, estimator status, checksums, and admission decision.

steps.title: Run the exact ONNX artifact in browser
steps.instruction: Load the exact exported policy into the production Studio and prove hover or waypoint behavior, inference rate, supervisor state, and replay hash.

steps.title: Exercise failure and recovery
steps.instruction: Record at least one bounded cancellation, retry, provider outage, or non-passing scorecard path without relabeling it success.

steps.title: Review scorecard and economics
steps.instruction: A reviewer confirms the passing threshold, actual artifact identity, reproducibility limits, wall time, execution backend, separate vehicle/host energy meanings, provider/electricity cost basis, recovery evidence, and any revised SLO.

milestones.title: External course and verified leaderboard acceptance
prerequisites: The course EnvSpec, scoring rules, task conversion, replay verifier, publication notice, and moderation path are versioned and public.
At least one competitor and the replay verifier are independent from the course author.
Leaderboard publication consent is explicit and current.
stopConditions: A score is published without server-side replay verification or current consent.
The verifier, course definition, or scoring rule changes without invalidating affected results.
A prohibited course bypasses the pre-generation safety guard or moderation path.
Raw identity, credential, or private replay data is copied into the public evidence manifest.

steps.title: Publish the versioned course
steps.instruction: Publish the admitted EnvSpec, task conversion, scoring rules, policy, provenance, and content hash.

steps.title: Complete independent discovery
steps.instruction: An external competitor finds and enters the course through the supported public surface without repository assistance.

steps.title: Submit a real replay
steps.instruction: The competitor completes the course and submits a replay bound to course, model, policy, and contract hashes.

steps.title: Verify the submission independently
steps.instruction: The server and independent verifier reproduce the result, reject tampering, and retain exact verification output.

steps.title: Publish consented leaderboard result
steps.instruction: Publish only the consented pseudonymous result and prove withdrawal or privacy controls remain available.

steps.title: Use the course directly as a training task
steps.instruction: Resolve the published course into the versioned training task without hand-copying or semantic drift.

steps.title: Exercise moderation and support
steps.instruction: Submit a bounded test report, exercise triage/appeal ownership, and measure response against the declared SLA.

milestones.title: Controlled D12 hardware lab acceptance
prerequisites: D30 remains accepted only for controlled D12 lab pilots; external beta is disabled.
The reference rover must complete the declared ladder before the reference quad attempt begins.
Lab mode, local provider, D12 rig allowlist, physical confirmation, supervisor, kill switch, incident owner, and telemetry consent are all current.
Props are removed for quad HITL and physical restraint is installed before tethered hover.
stopConditions: Any path attempts to auto-arm, elevate policy authority, skip physical confirmation, or use a non-D12 rig.
The supervisor, kill switch, telemetry, observer, physical boundary, or local authority path is unavailable.
A validator, config-diff, battery, geofence, speed, attitude, rate, replay-order, or hash gate fails.
The incident owner calls stop; evidence capture never outranks physical safety.

steps.title: Verify lab authority and stop conditions
steps.instruction: Record D30, lab mode, local provider, D12 rig, personnel, physical confirmation, consent, emergency plan, and signed preflight checklist.

steps.title: Complete rover SITL
steps.instruction: Validate the admitted reference rover, policy scorecard, export, supervisor configuration, and replay before hardware connection.

steps.title: Complete rover HITL
steps.instruction: With wheels off ground, review the config diff, confirm stop fallback, measure command rates, and prove no-auto-arm.

steps.title: Complete constrained rover run
steps.instruction: Use the bounded course, exercise supervisor veto, kill, reconnect, power-loss recovery, telemetry, replay, ghost, and system-ID review.

steps.title: Sign off rover evidence
steps.instruction: All required lab roles sign the rover record before any quad stage starts.

steps.title: Complete quad SITL
steps.instruction: Validate the admitted reference quad, policy scorecard, supervisor envelope, and replay before hardware connection.

steps.title: Complete props-removed quad HITL
steps.instruction: With props removed, review config diff, prove firmware authority, measure policy/supervisor rates, fallback, physical confirmation, and no-auto-arm.

steps.title: Complete tethered quad run
steps.instruction: Photographically attest restraint, exercise supervisor veto and kill, measure recovery, capture telemetry, verify replay, inspect ghost divergence, and review system-ID output.

steps.title: Review failures and incident readiness
steps.instruction: Record every stop, anomaly, fallback, missed deadline, reconnect, power loss, and incident escalation without deleting failed attempts.

steps.title: Complete signed controlled-lab record
steps.instruction: All required roles sign the final evidence pack; explicitly state that external hardware beta remains disabled.

milestones.title: Lawful structural-print handoff acceptance
prerequisites: The structural part belongs to an admitted model and passes the selected manufacturing profile.
The assembly license ledger, provider terms, spend ceiling, exporter integrity check, and restricted-geometry behavior are current.
The provider path is a quote/link handoff; no direct checkout or seller payout is implied.
stopConditions: DfM, license, attribution, restriction, manifest-hash, provider-destination, price, or profile validation fails.
Restricted geometry reaches the provider instead of an allowed envelope/link-out representation.
A stale, malformed, or unbounded provider response becomes purchase truth.
A quote is represented as an order, final price, or successful manufacture.

steps.title: Freeze admitted structural part
steps.instruction: Record model, part, revision, geometry, mass, material, provenance, and validator identity before manufacturing evaluation.

steps.title: Run DfM against an exact profile
steps.instruction: Measure wall, overhang, support, bed fit, orientation, and profile identity; failed checks remain failures.

steps.title: Apply license-aware export
steps.instruction: Derive assembly policy, emit attribution manifest where required, and substitute restricted geometry with the documented envelope/link-out.

steps.title: Verify artifact integrity
steps.instruction: Bind the lawful artifact, manufacturing profile, manifest, and provider request with content hashes and allowlisted metadata.

steps.title: Obtain a real provider quote link
steps.instruction: Execute the bounded provider sandbox/live handoff and retain quote URL, normalized price/currency, latency, expiry, terms, and response provenance.

steps.title: Exercise restricted and invalid handoffs
steps.instruction: Prove missing/contradictory license evidence, unsafe URLs, bad hashes, and malformed price/profile data fail before provider authority.

steps.title: Review quote usability and limitations
steps.instruction: A manufacturing reviewer confirms the provider received only lawful artifacts and records cost, latency, limitations, and whether physical manufacture remains unproven.

milestones.title: Independent publish-and-equip marketplace acceptance
prerequisites: G2, privacy/deletion, moderation, support, and the explicit external-beta gate are satisfied for this bounded test.
The publisher and equipper are unrelated; the equipper receives no repository or private-owner access.
Only an admitted, reviewable model with current license/provenance evidence may be listed.
stopConditions: A draft, rejected, prohibited, unlicensed, unreviewed, or unauthorized model becomes public.
The equipper receives publisher-private data, repository help, or bypass authority.
Pattern contribution, publication, policy use, or leaderboard consent is inferred from another purpose.
Moderation, takedown, appeal, export, or deletion ownership is unavailable.

steps.title: Verify publisher authority
steps.instruction: Record ownership, publication consent, pattern-contribution choice, current notices, and account export/deletion path.

steps.title: Verify admission and policy evidence
steps.instruction: Validate model, lockfile, catalog reviews, provenance, license/export policy, and prohibited-content guard before listing.

steps.title: Publish through the governed listing path
steps.instruction: Create, review, approve, and list the model while retaining owner/reviewer separation and audit history.

steps.title: Complete independent discovery
steps.instruction: An unrelated equipper discovers the listed model from the public surface without repository or publisher-private access.

steps.title: Equip and validate independently
steps.instruction: The equipper opens, equips, configures, validates, and inspects consequences using only supported product controls.

steps.title: Verify public and private artifact boundaries
steps.instruction: Confirm share/listing data are public as declared and owner review, jobs, account, and private blobs remain inaccessible.

steps.title: Exercise report, takedown, and appeal
steps.instruction: Run a bounded moderation drill with named owner, SLA, takedown, appeal, escalation, and repeat-infringer paths.

steps.title: Complete independent debrief
steps.instruction: Publisher and equipper record usability, correctness, privacy, moderation, and support findings; no payout or direct-checkout claim is created.

milestones.title: Field event to repair-evidence acceptance
prerequisites: A real Desktop-captured event belongs to the consenting owner and is bound to an admitted model, deployment, firmware/config, and timestamped contract hash.
Retention, export, deletion, sharing, and training-reuse choices are explicit and independent.
The system-ID proposal and repair sheet remain review artifacts, never automatic hardware authority.
stopConditions: Telemetry ownership, consent, integrity, time order, contract hash, or retention authority is missing or withdrawn.
A system-ID change auto-applies, bypasses validation, or worsens the declared held-out metric without explicit rejection.
Repair evidence invents damage, parts, price, license, or provider truth.
Raw identifying telemetry or secrets are copied into the repository evidence record.

steps.title: Capture a real field event
steps.instruction: Desktop records the real event with consent, owner, model/deployment hashes, firmware/config identity, monotonic time, and integrity evidence.

steps.title: Verify replay integrity
steps.instruction: Reproduce the tape against the exact twin, verify timestamp order and hashes, and retain explicit gaps or corrupt segments.

steps.title: Measure ghost divergence
steps.instruction: Render measured versus simulated motion and quantify divergence using the declared metric and time window.

steps.title: Generate a reviewable system-ID proposal
steps.instruction: Fit bounded parameters from authorized data and emit a patch with provenance, confidence, before/after held-out error, and affected assumptions.

steps.title: Accept or reject the proposal
steps.instruction: A reviewer explicitly accepts or rejects the patch after validation, compatibility, replay, and policy re-evaluation; no auto-apply is allowed.

steps.title: Produce impact and repair evidence
steps.instruction: Identify the bounded event window, affected assembly, inspection steps, sourced replacement parts, lawful quote links, and uncertainty.

steps.title: Review repair usefulness
steps.instruction: The owner and reviewer score whether the evidence changed inspection or repair action and record false positives, omissions, and unresolved uncertainty.

steps.title: Close data lifecycle and acceptance
steps.instruction: Verify retention, sharing, export, deletion, and training-reuse choices; record what remains in backups without overclaiming erasure.
